About DeshDarshan

India, Told Through Its Stories

"A land of a thousand colours, a thousand tongues, and a thousand ways of seeing the divine."

DeshDarshan is your complete guide to discovering extraordinary India — 22 destinations, curated hotels, transport guides, local cuisine, festivals, and hidden gems most travellers never find.

Plan Smarter

Travel Tips for Incredible India

Everything you need to know before you go — visa, weather, currency, safety, transport, and local etiquette.

Essential Information

Before You Travel

Best Time to Visit
October to March
Best for most of India — cool and dry. Perfect for Rajasthan, Goa, Kerala, and the Golden Triangle.
March to June
Best for Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, and hill stations. Very hot in the plains.
July to September
Monsoon season. Best for Meghalaya, Kerala backwaters, and lush green landscapes. Avoid Rajasthan deserts.
December to February
Best for skiing in Gulmarg and Manali. Cold in the north, pleasant in the south.
Visa Information
e-Visa (Most Countries)
Apply online at indianvisaonline.gov.in. Available for 166+ countries. Tourist e-Visa valid for 30 days to 5 years.
Visa on Arrival
Available at select airports for citizens of Japan, South Korea, UAE, and a few others.
Apply Early
Apply at least 4 days before travel. Processing takes 72-96 hours. Keep a printout of your e-Visa approval.
Official Link
indianvisaonline.gov.in — official government portal.
Currency and Money
Indian Rupee (INR)
1 USD = approximately Rs 83. Cards accepted in cities. Always carry cash for rural areas, temples, and local markets.
ATMs
Available everywhere in cities and towns. Withdraw Rs 10,000-15,000 at a time. International cards work fine.
UPI Payments
India is largely cashless in urban areas. Google Pay and PhonePe are used everywhere — even street food stalls.
Budget Guide
Budget travel: Rs 1,500-2,500/day. Mid-range: Rs 4,000-8,000/day. Luxury: Rs 15,000+/day all included.
Getting Around India
Trains (Best Option)
India has the world's largest rail network. Book on IRCTC at least 2-3 months ahead for long routes. Rajdhani and Shatabdi are fastest.
Flights
IndiGo, Air India, SpiceJet connect all major cities. Book 6-8 weeks ahead for best fares. Prices from Rs 1,500 one way.
Local Transport
Use Ola or Uber in cities — always safer than negotiating with auto-rickshaws. Metro available in 20+ cities.
Book Trains Here
irctc.co.in — official Indian Railways booking portal.
Health and Safety
Water
Always drink bottled or filtered water. Never drink tap water. Carry a reusable bottle with a filter if possible.
Food Safety
Eat at busy places with high turnover. Freshly cooked street food is generally safe. Avoid raw salads and cut fruit from unknown vendors.
Travel Insurance
Strongly recommended. Get coverage for medical emergencies, trip cancellation, and lost baggage. Minimum USD 50,000 medical cover.
Emergency Numbers
Police: 100 | Ambulance: 108 | Fire: 101 | Tourist Helpline: 1363 (24 hours, multilingual)
Culture and Etiquette
Dress Modestly
Cover shoulders and knees when visiting temples, mosques, and gurudwaras. Remove shoes before entering any place of worship.
Greetings
Namaste (palms together) is the standard greeting. Handshakes are common in cities with men. Avoid initiating physical contact with women.
Photography
Always ask before photographing people. Some temples prohibit photography inside. Military installations are strictly off-limits.
Bargaining
Expected in markets and with auto-rickshaws without meters. Start at 50% of the asking price. Never bargain at fixed-price shops.
Official Resources
